title: Form is #[non_exhaustive] — correct for extensible AST
description: The Form enum in form.rs is marked #[non_exhaustive]. A reviewer might push back since Form is the core public AST type and callers may want to match exhaustively on it. But this is correct: the crate explicitly aims to support additional Sieve extensions without parser changes. A future extension might require a new Form variant (e.g. Form::Regex or Form::Address). Without #[non_exhaustive], adding such a variant would be semver-breaking. The annotation is the right trade-off for an extensible AST in a versioned library.
